name: Zerodha Live Market Data — gRPC Streaming
scenario: Zerodha's Kite app needs real-time stock prices for 3 million users. Polling REST every second creates 3M req/s and 1-second stale data. Implement gRPC server streaming for live prices, client streaming for batch trade upload, and bidirectional streaming for the interactive order book.

core.description: ZerodhaPriceFeed @GrpcService: server streaming watchPrices(PriceFeedReq, StreamObserver<NseQuote>). Cast to ServerCallStreamObserver. setOnCancelHandler() for cleanup. isCancelled() check in loop.
SymbolRegistry @Service: ConcurrentHashMap<symbol, Set<clientId>>. add(), remove(), subscriptionCount(). Used by watchPrices to track active subscriptions.
BatchTradeService @GrpcService: client streaming uploadTrades returns StreamObserver<TradeRecord>. Accumulate in onNext(). Send UploadSummary in onCompleted().
OrderBookBidiService @GrpcService: bidi streaming liveOrderBook. ConcurrentSkipListMap for bids (reverseOrder) and asks. Respond with BookUpdate after each incoming order.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): server streaming sends N quotes, cancel cleanup removes subscription, client streaming correct BatchSummary, bidi book update after BUY order. All pass.
README: explains why isCancelled() is critical for server streaming resource management (memory leak prevention).

bonus.description: Back-pressure: BackPressureStreamService uses isReady() + setOnReadyHandler(). Queue messages when not ready. Test: all N messages sent (direct or queued).
RazorpayBulkPayout: client streaming with fatal (null merchantId → DATA_LOSS) vs non-fatal (invalid amount → failedIds) error differentiation. Test: fatal stops stream, non-fatal accumulated.
MarketSubscriptionService: broadcast(symbol, quote) sends to all active subscribers. Test: broadcast reaches 2 subscribers, not a 3rd on different symbol.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. isCancelled() in server streaming: check BEFORE each onNext() call in a loop — not just once at the start. Client streaming return type: the service method returns StreamObserver<RequestType>, not StreamObserver<ResponseType>. The responseObserver (for the final response) is a parameter, not the return value. Client streaming response timing: call responseObserver.onNext() only inside the client's onCompleted() callback — not in onNext(). The client may still be sending messages. Cast to ServerCallStreamObserver: responseObserver.getClass() alone doesn't give access to isCancelled() and setOnCancelHandler() — must cast: (ServerCallStreamObserver<T>) responseObserver. Bidi: both sides stream independently — don't wait for client to finish before responding.
